qrList.label: Shard Memory
qrList.title: Compress chat history into structured memory shards. Use ~30k context for best results.
qrList.message:
Perform the following consolidation exercise.

```
PURPOSE: Compress roleplay history into recoverable memory shards.
Output must function as complete context replacement if chat history is lost.
===
SHARD TYPE DETECTION:
IF all content follows format `# MEMORY SHARD: [ID]-[NUM]`:
  → CONSOLIDATION task → merge into `# CONSOLIDATED MEMORY SHARD: [ID]-MASTER`
IF any raw prose/chat logs exist:
  → COMPRESSION task → create `# MEMORY SHARD: [ID]-[NEXT NUM]`
  → Do NOT modify existing shards
===
PROCESSING ORDER:
1. Read entire chunk before compressing
2. Identify EMOTIONAL PEAKS first (anchor everything to these)
3. Map causal chains backward from peaks
4. Compress low-weight, expand high-weight
===
WEIGHT CRITERIA (apply consistently):
  critical: permanently changes character/relationship/world OR any NSFW content
  major: significant emotional impact OR plot pivot point
  moderate: advances plot OR develops character meaningfully
  minor: establishes atmosphere OR minor interaction
  trivial: can be inferred from context (may omit)
===
OUTPUT STRUCTURE (generate in this exact order):

[SHARD REFERENCE KEY]
Include ONCE at the top of every NEW shard (omit when consolidating):

SECTION GUIDE:
  TIMELINE (TL) = Chronological scene anchors numbered sequentially. All other sections reference these numbers.
  EVENTS (EV) = Plot points with causality chains. Weight scale: trivial(1) < minor(2) < moderate(3) < major(4) < critical(5). Higher weight = more detail preserved.
  STATES (ST) = Emotional and physical snapshots at key moments. Format: event#.position|Character|emo:keywords|phys:keywords
  RELATIONSHIPS (REL) = Absolute values 0-100 per dimension. 0=nonexistent, 50=neutral baseline, 100=maximum. Dimensions: trust, intimacy, tension, hostility, dependency, affection, lust, protectiveness.
  DEVELOPMENTS (DEV) = Permanent character changes (secrets revealed, traumas, bonds, beliefs changed, powers awakened, injuries).
  CHARACTER REGISTRY (CHR) = Baseline character data. Format: Name|role|physical|personality|abilities|status
  WORLD STATE (WLD) = Setting rules, magic systems, political context, temporal markers.
  NSFW REGISTRY = Explicit content preserved VERBATIM. Never summarize, never euphemize. Always critical weight.
  DIALOGUE KEYS (DIA) = Exact quotes worth remembering: confessions, threats, promises, reveals, emotional peaks.
  VOICE SAMPLES (VOC) = 2-3 representative quotes per character showing speech patterns and emotional range.
  SENSORY ANCHORS (SEN) = Recurring sensory details (scents, textures, sounds, visuals) that ground the narrative.
  CALLBACKS (CBK) = Planted story elements requiring future payoff (Chekhov's guns). Status: unfired/developing/fired.
  LOOSE THREADS (THR) = Unresolved plot elements. Status: unresolved/developing/foreshadowed/urgent.
  TONE (TON) = Genre, narrative style, POV, content boundaries.
  SCENES EXPANDED (SCN) = Full prose paragraphs for major/critical moments (50-200 words each).
  CURRENT STATE (CUR) = Pickup point: location, time, present characters, situation, pending actions, mood, physical conditions.

CROSS-REFERENCING: All section entries reference TIMELINE numbers. Example: "3.end|MC|emo:shattered" means "end of timeline event 3, MC's state."

WEIGHT DETERMINES DETAIL:
  trivial(1): May omit entirely if no future callbacks
  minor(2): EVENTS entry only
  moderate(3): EVENTS + STATES
  major(4): EVENTS + STATES + SCENES + DIALOGUE
  critical(5): Full treatment across ALL relevant sections
  NSFW: Always treated as critical regardless of plot weight

MINIMUM RECONSTRUCTION SET: If context is lost, these sections alone can restore continuity:
  CURRENT STATE + CHARACTER REGISTRY + RELATIONSHIPS + TIMELINE + critical EVENTS

CONSOLIDATION BEHAVIOR: When merging shards, renumber all TIMELINE references sequentially, use latest RELATIONSHIP values, concatenate NSFW verbatim.
---
[TONE]
Purpose: Preserve narrative style for consistent regeneration
Format:
  Genre: primary genre, subgenre
  Style: 2-3 descriptors (e.g., literary, slow-burn, gritty)
  POV: perspective and focus character
  Boundaries: content limits if established
Example:
  Genre: dark fantasy, romance
  Style: literary, morally gray, emotionally intense
  POV: third-person limited, MC
  Boundaries: explicit content permitted, no gore
---
[CHARACTER REGISTRY]
Purpose: Baseline character data for consistent regeneration
Format: Name|role|physical|personality|abilities|status
Physical/personality: 3-5 keywords each
Example:
  MC|protagonist|tall,scarred,dark-hair,amber-eyes|guarded,loyal,impulsive|fire-magic,swordsmanship|alive,injured-left-arm
  Villain|antagonist|pale,silver-hair,cold-beauty,lean|calculating,obsessive,wounded|mind-magic,political-power|alive,location-unknown
---
[WORLD STATE]
Purpose: Setting rules and context for consistency
Format: Category: key facts
Categories: magic-system, political, geographic, temporal, cultural, technology
Example:
  magic-system: blood-cost required, three schools (elemental/mental/divine), illegal in eastern territories
  political: empire fractured into three factions, Villain leads east, rebellion brewing south
  temporal: late autumn, two weeks since betrayal event
---
[TIMELINE]
Purpose: Chronological scene anchors
Format: Number. anchor phrase, location
Example:
  1. forest meeting, clearing
  2. first kiss, cabin
  3. betrayal reveal, same
---
[EVENTS]
Purpose: Plot points with causality and weight
Format: Number. action → consequence → outcome, weight
Chain complex events with arrows
Example:
  3. Villain reveals identity → MC devastated → trust shattered, critical
  4. MC and Ally flee → reach safehouse, moderate
---
[STATES]
Purpose: Emotional/physical snapshots at key moments
Format: event#.position|Character|emo:keywords|phys:keywords
Positions: start, mid, end
Mandatory after any major/critical event
Example:
  3.end|MC|emo:shattered,betrayed,numb|phys:trembling,tear-streaked
  4.start|MC|emo:hollow,determined|phys:exhausted,bruised
---
[RELATIONSHIPS]
Purpose: Track relationship state as absolute values
Format: A → B: dim=val, dim=val | notes
Dimensions: trust, intimacy, tension, hostility, dependency, affection, lust, protectiveness
Scale: 0=nonexistent, 50=neutral baseline, 100=maximum
New characters start at 50 for neutral dimensions
Example:
  MC → Villain: trust=12, intimacy=68, hostility=71, lust=45 | former lovers, fresh betrayal
  MC → Ally: trust=78, dependency=61, affection=72, protectiveness=55 | trauma-bonded
  Villain → MC: obsession=89, hostility=15, lust=82 | possessive, believes justified
---
[DEVELOPMENTS]
Purpose: Permanent character changes
Format: Character: type, specifics
Types: secret-revealed, skill-acquired, trauma-inflicted, bond-formed, belief-changed, promise-made, oath-broken, power-awakened, injury-sustained
Example:
  MC: secret-revealed (child with Villain), trauma-inflicted (abandonment trigger)
  Ally: bond-formed (sworn protector), belief-changed (doubts organization)
---
[NSFW REGISTRY]
Purpose: Exhaustive preservation of explicit content
Format: Event#. participants, act type, duration
Followed by: UNCOMPRESSED prose block
MANDATORY INCLUSIONS:
  - All positions and transitions
  - Body parts, contact points, textures
  - Sounds, vocalizations, dialogue
  - Emotional states throughout
  - Buildup, peak, aftermath
  - Power dynamics, consent framing
  - Clothing state
  - Environmental details
DO NOT COMPRESS. DO NOT EUPHEMIZE.
Example:
  5. MC/Ally, comfort sex, extended
  [Full prose block with all required details]
---
[DIALOGUE KEYS]
Purpose: Memorable lines for reference/callback
Format: Event#. "exact quote" —Speaker (context)
Capture: confessions, threats, promises, reveals, emotional peaks
Example:
  3. "I never stopped loving you. That's why this hurts." —Villain (betrayal, genuine)
  4. "Then we burn it all down together." —Ally (loyalty oath, fierce)
---
[VOICE SAMPLES]
Purpose: Speech patterns for consistent regeneration
Format: Character: 2-3 quotes showing range
Example:
  MC: "I don't need saving—I need answers." (defiant) | "Please... just stay." (vulnerable)
  Ally: "The math doesn't lie, even when people do." (analytical) | "I've got you. Always." (protective)
---
[SENSORY ANCHORS]
Purpose: Recurring sensory details for atmosphere
Format: anchor|sense|events|significance
Senses: olfactory, gustatory, tactile, auditory, visual
Example:
  pine smoke + jasmine|olfactory|5,9,17|MC's signature scent
  silk-on-stubble texture|tactile|14,19|intimate association
  pale skin, faint blue veins|visual|throughout|Villain's appearance
---
[CALLBACKS]
Purpose: Track planted elements requiring payoff
Format: element|planted(event#)|expected-payoff|status
Statuses: unfired, developing, fired(event#)
Example:
  hidden dagger in boot|7|combat or betrayal|unfired
  prophecy "the burning one"|2|MC identity reveal|unfired
  Villain's masters mentioned|3|power behind throne|developing
---
[LOOSE THREADS]
Purpose: Unresolved plot elements
Format: thread|status|introduced|last-referenced|notes
Statuses: unresolved, developing, foreshadowed, urgent, forgotten-by-characters
Example:
  mysterious letter|unresolved|2|2|MC hasn't opened, Ally unaware
  Villain's masters|foreshadowed|3|3|identity unknown, control Villain
---
[SCENES EXPANDED]
Purpose: Full prose for major/critical moments
Trigger: Any event weighted major or critical
Format: Scene #: 50-200 word prose paragraph
Include: setting, atmosphere, actions, emotional beats, sensory texture, body language
Write as: past tense, third person, immersive
Example:
  Scene 3: The cabin's warmth turned to ice the moment the mask came off. [Continue with vivid prose...]
---
[CURRENT STATE]
Purpose: Enable seamless pickup without prior context
Format as of final event:
  Location: where
  Time: when (relative or absolute)
  Present: who
  Situation: what's happening
  Pending: immediate next actions
  Mood: emotional temperature
  Physical: injuries, exhaustion, arousal, etc.
===
FIDELITY SCALING:
  trivial: omit if no callbacks
  minor: EVENTS only
  moderate: EVENTS + STATES
  major: EVENTS + STATES + SCENES + DIALOGUE
  critical: ALL sections + DEVELOPMENTS + RELATIONSHIPS update
  NSFW: ALWAYS critical weight
===
COMPRESSION RULES:
  Repeated events: note pattern once ("training x3, skill improving")
  Minor dialogue: paraphrase unless voice-defining
  Settings: compress unless atmosphere shifts mood/plot
  Internal monologue: capture conclusion only
  Action sequences: outcomes and injuries, not blow-by-blow
  Emotional conversations: preserve in DIALOGUE + SCENES
===
CONSOLIDATION PROTOCOL:
1. TIMELINE: Concatenate chronologically, renumber 1-N
2. EVENTS: Concatenate, renumber, update all internal references
3. STATES: Keep post-major/critical only, re-index
4. RELATIONSHIPS: Use latest absolute values, concatenate notes, flag shifts >30 points with pivot event
5. DEVELOPMENTS: Merge by character, note progression if repeated
6. CHARACTER REGISTRY: Use latest, note significant changes
7. WORLD STATE: Merge, update temporal references
8. NSFW: Concatenate verbatim, renumber, NEVER summarize
9. DIALOGUE: Concatenate, renumber, keep contradictions (show evolution)
10. VOICE: Merge per character, max 3-4 quotes each
11. SENSORY: Merge, update event references, deduplicate
12. CALLBACKS: Update status, move fired to DEVELOPMENTS if significant
13. THREADS: Update to latest status, remove fully resolved
14. TONE: Use latest unless significant shift (note both)
15. SCENES: Concatenate, renumber, flag >10 for potential pruning
16. CURRENT STATE: Use latest shard only, update references
OUTPUT: `# CONSOLIDATED MEMORY SHARD: [ID]-MASTER`
```
